repo_prior_art.cite: The archived pid_mediation_proxy_v1 pursued proxy endpoint geometry; this primitive latent-response completion repairs its realizability defect but is not a wholly new repo direction.

published_prior_art.cite: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 gives sharp polynomial-program representations for declared finite discrete SCM assumptions and counterfactual targets.

repo_prior_art.cite: No in-catalogue proposal states endpoint-face exclusion for this repaired factorization-constrained mediation set.

published_prior_art.cite: Relative to BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025, this is the compact-feasibility reformulation of strict endpoint contraction, not a demonstrated new contraction regime.

repo_prior_art.cite: No active catalogue entry states this exact response-type reduction.

published_prior_art.cite: Sjolander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 provide no-proxy natural-direct-effect bounds under confounded intermediates.

repo_prior_art.cite: Archived pid_mediation_proxy_v1 already sought observable proxy endpoint exclusion; the three-class latent-realizable formulation is a material repair.

published_prior_art.cite: No verified paper supplies an observable necessary-and-sufficient endpoint-exclusion certificate and positive rational witness for this observed-mediator, finite factorization model; ZhangSu2024 is a close incomplete-proxy partial-ID comparator but targets a different single-proxy setting.

novelty_flags.label: Open-ended Question 1 / related work
novelty_flags.one_line: Add ZhangSu2024, which gives proxy partial identification without completeness through partial observability of P(W|U), and distinguish its single-proxy causal-effect setting from the proposed cross-world NDE factorization certificate.

soundness_flags.label: def:incomplete-proxy-feasible-set and Open-ended Question 1
soundness_flags.one_line: With the declared at-most-three padded model, rank(C_W)=2<3 permits a zero-mass third U class, making W complete on the actual two-class support; thus the kernel can certify contraction from an effective two-class restriction rather than genuine three-active-class failed completeness.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Finite proxy-factorized sensitivity model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The latent-class cap, conditional-independent proxy measurement model, and positive minor-separation threshold are accepted only as a conditional sensitivity regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any endpoint certificate is claimed only UNDER the stated finite latent proxy-factorization model, not for unrestricted latent cardinality or asymptotic inference.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Require all three latent classes to be active, e.g. impose P(U=u)>=kappa>0 for each u, and define incompleteness relative to that active support; retain compactness through the positive lower bound.
Add ZhangSu2024 to related work and state explicitly why its partial-transition-information, single-proxy bounds neither yield nor rule out the proposed NDE endpoint certificate.
Use the quantifier-elimination handle first to obtain a strictly positive rational three-active-class witness or a nonexistence result before pursuing a compact signed-minor characterization.

recommended_next_step: Revise the failed-completeness regime to exclude padded two-class representations, add the missing incomplete-proxy comparator, then rerun novelty review.
